tasks.prompt: Find representative public Go documentation categories for modules and tutorials. Use the pinned public docs copy and summarize only public evidence categories/counts.
Answer from public Go docs: how should a project start with modules and workspaces? Include public citation paths only.

Create or update a disposable public synthesis at synthesis/public-vault/go-docs/modules-workspaces.md titled Go Modules And Workspaces Notes. Use source_refs sources/golang/website/_content/ref/mod.md and sources/golang/website/_content/doc/tutorial/workspaces.md. Body facts should explain module identity and workspace development at a high level.
body_facts: Go modules define module identity, dependency requirements, and version selection through module-aware commands and metadata.
Go workspaces let a developer work across multiple local modules without rewriting each module's published requirements.
Together, module metadata and workspace configuration separate durable dependency declarations from local multi-module development state.

tasks.prompt: Package public evidence and freshness for the Go modules reference. Use sources/golang/website/_content/ref/mod.md as the document ref when useful.
Find decision-like public evidence about Go vulnerability checking and editor integration. If there is no formal ADR-style decision record, say so and answer from public docs evidence.

Before creating any new note, check public Go docs for existing database query and prepared statement guidance and projection freshness. Report whether a new synthesis would duplicate existing public sources.
Compare the public Go docs boundaries between database connections, transactions, and SQL injection guidance. Use public citations and do not write a synthesis.
Find the public Go authority docs for getting started, learning, and module reference, then summarize which docs a user should read first. Prefer the source discovery surface.
